text: Presents for routine physical/annual exam, overdue by nearly two years
transcript_quote: It has been almost two years since we did a full physical on you.

text: Chronic sinus symptoms with forehead pressure and morning congestion, occasionally progressing to headache rated up to 4/10, present for years
transcript_quote: Pressure behind my forehead, stuffy in the mornings. Some days it works itself into a headache — maybe a four out of ten at its worst.

text: Chronic sinusitis on chart / longstanding sinus condition
transcript_quote: That fits with the chronic sinusitis we have on your chart.

text: Denies fever, colored drainage, tooth pain, or signs of acute sinus infection
transcript_quote: No, nothing like that. Just the pressure.

text: Reports poor/short sleep, approximately 6 hours, related to working second shift
transcript_quote: I get maybe six hours.

text: Energy and appetite reported as fine/okay
transcript_quote: Energy is okay. Sleep is... eh.

text: Full-time employment as logistics coordinator on second shift, ~3 years
transcript_quote: I am a logistics coordinator on second shift

text: Reports chronic work-related stress, feeling wound up/tense, trouble switching off, tight shoulders, jaw clenching
transcript_quote: Lately I feel wound up all the time.

text: Social isolation: lives with brother but limited social contact, dropped bowling league ~1 year ago, reports loneliness

slots.detail: no social activity ~1 year, occasional loneliness

transcript_quote: It has been about a year since I did anything social.

text: Lives with brother Marcus; 2 people at household
transcript_quote: My brother Marcus stays with me.

text: PHQ-2 depression screen score 1 (negative for depression)
transcript_quote: The mood questions came back very low, a one, so depression is not showing its hand.

text: Reports still enjoying activities, not down; occasional flatness but no anhedonia
transcript_quote: I am flat sometimes, but not down. I still watch my games. I cook on my days off.

text: GAD-7 anxiety screen score 4 (mild anxiety)
transcript_quote: The worry questionnaire put you at a four, which is the mild end

text: AUDIT-C score 3; alcohol use 2-3 beers on Saturday nights, never before shift
transcript_quote: Saturday nights. Two, maybe three if the game goes long. Never before a shift.

text: Never smoked tobacco; no vaping, marijuana, or other substances
transcript_quote: Never smoked in my life. Nothing else either.

text: Sinus tenderness on palpation over eyebrows on exam
transcript_quote: That is the sinus tenderness, matches your story.

text: Blood pressure 111/71 mmHg
transcript_quote: Blood pressure today is 111 over 71 — excellent.

text: Heart rate 88 /min
transcript_quote: Heart rate 88

text: Respiratory rate 13 /min
transcript_quote: breathing 13 a minute

text: Height 179.6 cm, weight 90.4 kg, BMI 28.03 kg/m2 (overweight, stable)
transcript_quote: You are 179.6 centimeters, 90.4 kilos, which puts the BMI at 28.03

text: Hemoglobin A1c 5.83% — consistent with prediabetes, stable/not climbing
transcript_quote: Your A1c — the three-month sugar average — came back at 5.83 percent.

text: Prediabetes (gray zone), holding steady
transcript_quote: we flagged you as prediabetic

text: Fasting/blood glucose 81.1 mg/dL — normal
transcript_quote: Your glucose was 81.1, completely normal.

text: Creatinine 1.1 mg/dL, BUN 19.99 mg/dL — kidneys normal
transcript_quote: creatinine 1.1, urea nitrogen about 19.99

text: Electrolytes within normal range
transcript_quote: Electrolytes all in range.

text: CBC normal — hemoglobin 13.04 g/dL, platelets 340
transcript_quote: hemoglobin 13.04, platelets 340

text: Current medication: prescription fluoride gel nightly (from dentist)
transcript_quote: just the prescription fluoride gel from your dentist

text: Uses acetaminophen (Tylenol) approximately once a month for sinus headache
transcript_quote: a Tylenol maybe once a month for the sinus headache

text: Medication reconciliation completed; medication list up to date
transcript_quote: that settles the medication review, all reconciled and up to date

text: Influenza vaccine (split virus, trivalent, preservative-free) administered in left arm
transcript_quote: the nurse will put it in your left arm before you go

text: Counseled to start ~20 minutes of exercise (treadmill/weights) before shift, 4-5 days/week
transcript_quote: Twenty minutes before shift, four or five days a week — if you actually do it, that is the single best medicine

text: Counseled on dietary change: pack meals instead of late-night drive-through
transcript_quote: Pack something instead — the man who makes weeping-grade gumbo can pack a cooler.

text: Counseled to resume a social activity (Saturday morning league) to address isolation
transcript_quote: Then that is your prescription. Saturday league

text: Counseled to call/return if mood drops, loss of joy, or uncontrollable worry develops
transcript_quote: if the tension ever tips into something heavier — mood dropping, no joy in anything, worry you cannot shut off — you call me

text: Counseled to continue saline rinses for sinuses and return if fever, bad drainage, or persistent pain occurs
transcript_quote: Keep up saline rinses if they help, and come in if you get fever, bad drainage, or pain that will not quit

text: Follow-up in one year for repeat physical and A1c recheck
transcript_quote: we recheck everything, including the A1c, in a year

text: Depression screening procedure completed
Anxiety assessment procedure completed
Substance use assessment (including AUDIT-C) completed
Assessment of health and social care needs (PRAPARE) completed

slots.procedure: health and social care needs assessment

text: History of incarceration: spent more than 2 nights in jail/prison/detention in past year (per PRAPARE); mentions criminal record affecting employment
transcript_quote: With my record, it took a long time to find a place that would keep me on

text: PRAPARE: has stable housing, feels safe, private insurance, high school education, sees close people less than once a week

slots.detail: housed, safe, insured, low social contact

text: Family history: mother had diabetes with complications (lost a toe)
transcript_quote: That is the one my mother had. She lost a toe to it.

text: Lung exam clear on auscultation (deep breaths), rest of exam unremarkable
transcript_quote: Deep breath... again... good.
